programmes.show_description: Alf loses his job and comes home to find he may have been burgled. It's an unhappy birthday but a surprise is in store. S7 E6: Unemployment

A US Army sergeant is killed as he tries to stop an armed robbery, but all might not be as it seems when the man's girlfriend is involved in the gang that committed the crime. S3 E11: The Hi-Jackers

In 1933, sightings began of a creature in Loch Ness. This programme examines evidence and rare footage to explore an enduring mystery. Narrated by Robert Powell.

The East End is rocked by the Luftwaffe in 1940 as whole areas are reduced to rubble; it emerges victorious into a new world, but the Docks are closed and the old East End will vanish within 30 years.

The B-25, often called 'the forgotten bomber', the mighty North American twin delivered the first blow in the fight back against Japan, in one of the most daring and audacious raids of all time. S2 E3

In 1943, the Nazis looted $50 million in gold from the Jewish community in Djerba in Tunisia; the loot is tracked to the family of an escaped Nazi in South America. S4 E2: The Mystery of Rommel's Gold
The real fighting begins as James and Mike travel inland and analyse the Canadians' first encounters with the Waffen SS in a forgotten but vital battle. S1 E2: Attrition

programmes.show_name: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programmes.show_description: Documentary. This episode shows how the Nazi invasion of the Soviet Union in June 1941 was a crucial catalyst for the radicalisation of the Nazi policy against the Jews. Ep5: The Road To Treblinka
The development and operational history of the best and most famous fighter aircraft of World War II, some of which changed the course of the conflict and led to the beginnings of new aerial warfare.
At the outbreak of WWII, the only heavy four-engined bombers in the war were either Russian or American - soon the Short Stirling, the Avro Lancaster, and the Liberator were added. Ep6: Heavy Bombers
There are some news events that change the world. We look at the war in Afghanistan and the attempts to defeat the Taliban.
George Michael's slide into self-destruction led to his sudden death at 53. Now expert pathologist, Dr Michael Hunter, investigates what was going on in the singer's body that caused him to die.
After years of captivity, Allied POW's prepare for liberation. But In the bitter winter of January 1945, prisoners are ordered out at gunpoint. It is the beginning of the Long March. Ep3: Freedom
We begin the countdown of America's most notorious unsolved mysteries and crimes by exploring who killed Marilyn Monroe and ask if one of America's most infamous killers got away with murder. E1
When Jack visits Sir Manners - the father of his late officer - and gives him a present, Manners offers him a job; elsewhere, Jack saves Tom from a beating. S1 E4: Swords and Pick Handles
When reformed Ron the Burglar manages to open Arthur's lock-up, Arthur realises it's time for Daley Enterprises to strengthen its warehouse security. S8 E8: Him Indoors
Rigsby attempts to have himself cast as male lead in a play written by effeminate lodger Hilary when he discovers that Ruth is playing the role of the love interest. S3 E2: Stage Struck
The students prepare to take their exams at the end of the first term, but are they ready? S1 E13: The Examination
Gene and the team must protect an undercover policewoman investigating a drugs ring on their patch, and Alex continues to be haunted by the ghostly constable. S3 E4
The story of bad tempered Alf Garnett who has not mellowed with the years and is as bigoted as ever. S1 E1
With Suzi Quatro and Nick Lowe. The legendary BAFTA-winning music and comedy show from Thames TV (1978). All in the best possible taste!
A crooked philanthropist tries to get his hands on a Puerto Rican farmer's land, but Simon is determined to help the farmer keep it. S3 E12: The Unkind Philanthropist

On 26 April 1986 the worst nuclear accident in history occurred. This is the detailed build up to how that disaster happened. Narrated by Robert Powell.
The first decade of the 20th century would see what was known as the 'Golden Age' of railways when locomotive size would double and the railways would reach their zenith. We follow its development.
The many variants of the A-1 Skyraider single piston-engined fighter bomber helped dominate the air to ground attack role in Vietnam and earned quite the reputation. S2 E4
Hitler had a dream to amass the world's greatest art collection; the team investigates how Hitler oversaw the theft of thousands of art treasures from across Europe. S4 E3: Hitler's Obsession
James and Mike range deep into Normandy, where key later engagements took place, as they visit neglected battlegrounds and absorb the immense scale of the campaign. S1 E3: Breakout

programmes.show_name: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programmes.show_description: Examining the rise of the Nazis and their impact. Fear and hatred of Bolshevism drove many Germans to fight to the bitter end despite continued suffering. Ep6: Fighting To The End
To celebrate the centenary of the Royal Air Force, Ewan and Colin McGregor meet people from throughout its history and take to the skies in some of the world's most iconic planes.
Battleships were seen as the key weapon at sea at the beginning of World War II, but their vulnerability was exposed by submarines and aircraft over the next six years. Ep7: The Battleship
There are some news events that change the world; we go back to the 20th of July, 1969 and take a look at man's quest to land on the Moon.
In 1971, Jim Morrison was one of the world's most famous singers, but he was at war with inner demons. Using archive footage and interviews with friends, we examine the events leading to his death.
A look at the rise and fall of the London docks, once the commercial hub of Britain during the days of the old Empire, and enabled riches to be brought to the capital with a constant stream of ships.
The countdown of America's most infamous mysteries continues and looks at General Custer's last stand, explores who whacked Bugsy Sigel, and investigates a deadly attack on Wall Street in 1920. E2
The strike continues as Bill works out that a coal seam runs under his house, and Tom pursues two kids who have stolen from him but takes pity on them when he discovers why. S1 E5: Coal Comfort
Arthur plans to boost the membership of the Winchester Club, but ends up in trouble over a stolen karaoke machine. S8 E9: The Greatest Show in Willesden
Rigsby turns up at the house with a new sports car apparently purchased in yet another attempt to woo Miss Jones, but his driving doesn't appear to be as stylish as his motor. S3 E3: Clunk Click
At the start of a new school year, Mr. Brown has two new students... as well as ten old ones who did not pass their exam. S2 E1: All Present If Not Correct
DCI Gene Hunt immediately smells a rat when his former Manchester colleagues DCI Litton and DI Bevan turn up on his patch. S3 E5
